sections.paragraph:
μωρία, ας, ἡ (μωρός; Soph., Hdt. et al.; PBrem 61, 28 [II a.d.]; PCairMasp 4, 6 Byz.; Sir 20:31; 41:15; Philo; Jos., Ant. 17, 209; Iren. 1, 16, 3 [Harv. I, 162, 2]) foolishness gener. of worldly wisdom (Orig., C. Cels. 7, 47, 9) μ. παρὰ τῷ θεῷ ἐστιν 1 Cor 3:19. Conversely, to all those who are lost 1:18 and esp. to the gentiles vs. 23, the Christian preaching of a Savior who died a slave’s death on the cross was μ. (cp. Theoph. Ant. 2, 1 [p. 94, 7]). It has pleased God to save the believers διὰ τ. μωρίας τοῦ κηρύγματος vs. 21. The ψυχικὸς ἄνθρ. rejects the things of the spirit as μ., 2:14. The Judean temple cult is evaluated as μ. (opp. θεοσέβεια) Dg 3:3.—WCaspari, Über d. bibl. Begriff der Torheit: NKZ 39, 1928, 668–95.—DELG s.v. 1 μωρός. TW. Spicq.

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

PBrem PBrem = Die Bremer Papyri—List 4

PCairMasp PCairMasp = JMaspero, Papyrus grecs d’époque byzantine—List 4

Byz. Byz. = Byzantine, esp. in ref. to papyri

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Iren. Iren. = Irenaeus, Haereses, II a.d.—List 5

Harv. Harv. = WHarvey; s. Iren.—List 5

gener. gener. = generally

Orig Orig , var. works, II–III a.d.—List 5

esp. esp. = especially

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Theoph. Ant. Theoph. Ant. = Theophilus Antiochenus, II a.d.—List 5

opp. opp. = opposed to, opposite

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

NKZ NKZ = Neue Kirchliche Zeitschrift—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 663
